title: 'White Noise - The Tower | First Complete Draft 07/27/16'

blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'

categories:

  - 'White Noise'
---

<br /><h3>So... I completed the first draft of my short novel White Noise - The Tower (Episode 1).&nbsp;</h3><br />You can read it (warts and all) below. I'm going to go back through and start editing (I already have). But it's mostly done now. I've also roughed out a book cover (we'll see if I stick with this direction).<br /><br />Of course, I'm taking a big risk by calling it "Episode 1"... that means I have to write Episode 2, 3, &amp; 4 at least. (That's where I have the story sketched out to at this point.<br /><br />I hope you enjoy!<br /><br /><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/white-noise-the-tower-cover-e8ba5451650c.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="460" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/white-noise-the-tower-cover-8b75ac54f106.jpg" width="640" /></a></div><br /><br /><br />White Noise - The Tower<br /><br />D.G. Wolfe<br /><br />This is a work of fiction. Names, characters, businesses, places, events and incidents are either the products of the authorâ€™s imagination or used in a fictitious manner. Any resemblance to actual persons, living or dead, or actual events is purely coincidental.<br /><br />Copyright Â© 2016 D.G. Wolfe<br />All rights reserved.<br />ISBN-13: 978-1535470070<br />ISBN-10: 1535470070<br /><br />Cover Illustration Copyright Â© YYYY by xxxxx<br />Cover design by xxxxxxx<br />Editing by xxxxx<br />Chapter opening illustrations Â© YYYY by xxxxx<br />Author photograph by xxxxxxxx<br /><br /><br />To Jesus, who asked me to tell him a story.<br />To Flavia, who supported me despite myself.<br />To others, who kept pushing me to be more of who I was created to be.<br />Who Am I?<br /><br />My name is Darrell G. Wolfe<br /><br />Story Teller | Creative | INFJ | Intellection | Learner | Ideation | Achiever | Input | Multipotentialite<br /><br />I make complicated things simple; through writing, storytelling, &amp; studies. <br /><br />My fiction is published under D.G. Wolfe to draw a clear line between my fiction and non-fiction work.<br /><br />Check out more of my books, blogs, writing, and more at DarrellWolfe.Com<br /><br /><br />Other things about me:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>I am a husband.<br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>I am a father of two home schooled boys.<br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>I am a wealth of all kinds of random information, and I make complicated things simple.<br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>I am a born again spirit-led, creative, intellectual, Christian.<br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>I am the son of an Atheist Pastor.<br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>I am the son of a Nurse.<br /><br /><br />&nbsp;Street Noise<br /><br /><br />Out of the corner of his eye, through the window of The Grounded Cafe, Ash saw two men dressed in black combat gear duck behind a car.<br /><br />Coolness rushed down his spine, and his muscles tensed.<br /><br />He reached for his side arm.<br /><br />He shook his head, and looked again, they were gone.<br /><br />Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, PTSD. That was the diagnosis theyâ€™d assigned him. Ash relaxed and sank back into the leather bound chair; alone, in the corner of his favorite coffee shop. Always keep your back to the wall, be near the most exits, but never too visible. It was like breathing, it never stopped... even in civilian life.<br /><br />As Dean of Military Science, Ash's job primarily lay in administrative work and policy these days, plus a few sessions counseling the younger cadets, and would-be trouble makers. He was quite overqualified for the position, but he ran a tight unit.<br /><br />He stared at the faces of the pedestrians as they passed by the window. Hurried. Busy. Buried in their smart phones. So oblivious to the world around them, or the little clouds that float around their shoulders.<br /><br />The leaves had begun to fall, most had turned shades of orange and yellow, matching perfectly the Pumpkins that had been dotting porches and windows through the city the past few weeks.<br /><br />Some commotion outside broke his train of thought... Everyone was stopping, turning, and staring in the same direction, a few moving toward the distraction.<br /><br />Ash stepped outside to see what was happening, turned a corner, and nearly ran into a bystander. A brisk evening breeze broke past his collar.<br /><br />A man in a tattered and dirty suit with a graying beard, was standing on top of a pickup truck screaming at no body in particular.<br /><br />"You always did love Mother best... I know... That's why... You couldn't keep your mouth shut..." On he went, incoherently.<br /><br />Just as Ash took a step closer, the man turned to stare him directly in the eye.<br /><br />In a deeper, gravelly voice: "You... What do you want with us?"<br /><br />Ash took a step back.<br /><br />Then the man turned back to his one sided conversation.<br /><br />Ash could see a large creature behind the man. The bottom half was more mist than form, as though it were made of smoke. The top half was like a man, with the head of a bull. It was charcoal, and streaks of white painted across the chest.<br /><br />Ash could feel his palms turn cold, and he felt as though he couldn't move his legs.<br /><br />A smaller creature was on the homeless man's shoulders, screaming into his ears. He couldn't hear the conversation, but Ash could tell that the homeless man was responding, and that he had only been hearing one half of the conversation. Ash looked around and nobody else seemed too surprised, as usual.<br /><br />The large creature stared directly at Ash, cocked his head to one side and disappeared along with the smaller one. The homeless man was by himself again, then his eyes narrowed. He leapt off the truck, and ran at Ash.<br /><br />It took all his training to keep from being pinned down. The old man was wiry but stronger than he looked.<br /><br />Ash let out a cry, "Jesus!"<br /><br />The wiry man stepped back, his eyes darted around, as though he looked confused.<br /><br />That was all Ash needed. In 30 seconds he had the man hog tied with his own belt.<br /><br />The police were just arriving. "Charlie's at it again... Let's take him in."<br /><br />Three big men in uniform put cuffs and a muzzle on the old man, and threw him, rather harshly, into the back of the squad car.<br /><br />Ash heard someone behind him, "Those were some moves Marine."<br /><br />"Airman. Uh... Thanks. No harm done." Ash noticed his cup crushed beneath him, the ice, coffee and cream covered the sidewalk, and his jeans. "Well.. Almost no harm anyway."<br /><br />"Why don't you let me get you anotherâ€¦ on me."<br /><br />Ash looked up to see a balding man in blue slacks, yellow polo, and a pile of papers in his arms. A logo with two E's &nbsp;overlapping bore prominently on his chest.<br /><br />"The least I could do for a man who'd be willing to step in to a situation like that, protect all these people. What's your name son?"<br /><br />"Ash. I need to get some new clothes. I don't think I need any more caffeine right now. But thanks for the offer." With a waive, he set off toward home.<br /><br />Ash headed down the side walk, past the shops and restaurants. There is a girl crying, typing away at the phone. A little red frog on her shoulder, was yelling into her ear. Ash could only imagine what it must be saying.<br /><br />A little old lady is sitting inside a window at the nursing home. She is knitting something, probably a blanket. A large man in all white, stands behind her, smiling. He looks up at Ash, his smile fades slightly, and then returns. He looks down to her again.<br /><br />Ash could not see the figure accompanying each person, only certain ones, here and there. Most were just clouds, or mist.<br /><br />"Don't worry so much." His doctor told him, "These may be after effects from the war. Weâ€™re just beginning to understand PTSD. The mind is a marvelous adapter to stress. As long as these phantoms do not interrupt your daily work, or cause you to want to harm yourself or others, you should be fine. Consider yourself lucky. Just think of them as a construct of your active imagination, an amusing distraction from the mundane realities of life."<br /><br />He turned off the street &nbsp;to an alley between two old buildings, and up a well-worn path into the woods, his refuge from the masses.<br /><br />Ash opened the door of his cabin.<br /><br />It wasn't a mansion by any means, but it was more than sufficient for him. 900 sq. ft., one large open room, a loft, a balcony.<br /><br />Yes sir, he had a fine cabin in the woods. All to himself. How he'd managed to live here eight years and still not have a single friend invited over was a mystery, even to himself. Then again, you'd have to have a friend to invite one.<br /><br />He liked things just so. He liked the solitude.<br /><br />At least that's what he told himself.<br /><br />Ash took a seat on his balcony, overlooking the hills and lake. He stared at the expansive forests, lost in time. The clock read: 6:00pm, he laid down in his hammock, bundled in the overpriced winter resistant sleeping bag, and watched the stars from the balcony until he drifted off.<br /><br />As sleep overtook him, he thought to himself "Yes sir, this is one fine cabin."<br /><br />***<br /><br />An explosion knocks him against a wall. He barely sees, through the smoke, troops running about, hauling rubble off of bodies, checking pulses. Some are screaming in pain. Others shouting to one another. Half the mess hall is completely gone, all that is left is fire and rubble.<br /><br />Ash can feel the beat of his heart in his neck, his forehead rushed with sweat. He sees them all running around, but all he hears is intense ringing.<br /><br />As the numbness subsides from his brain, shock waves hit his chest as more buildings are getting hit nearby.<br /><br />Ash uses the wall to steady himself, and works to stand on his feet.<br /><br />Others will tend to the wounded. This band of Jihadist miscreants found upgraded weapons; Russian no doubt.<br /><br />Ash shoots out the building, down the road to the artillery unit. He grabs his rifle and heads for the highest structure, a radio tower toward the side of the camp.<br /><br />He can see the launch area where the rockets are being fired from. He takes a few quick breaths, hyperventilating floods the lungs with oxygen, one last deep breath in and hold, steadies his body.<br /><br />Through the scope he can see about 10 of them.<br /><br />They only have two missile launchers. They have very little cover, firing from open desert, mostly hiding behind their Jeep, and a few large outcroppings of fallen mountainside.<br /><br />He fires two rounds back to back, taking out both missile launchers, one exploding in the handlers face. Ash then begins firing one round after another as the attackers fall like Coke cans on a fence post. The rounds hit their targets with extreme prejudice.<br /><br />Three remain, cowering behind the Jeep. Jeeps are great vehicles for driving through tough terrain quickly, but they are built light, not armored. He sees one peek through the glass, gone.<br /><br />The other two attempt to climb in the vehicle keeping their heads down and drive away. The problem with that, is that both seats are occupied. He doesn't need to see them.<br /><br />Two.<br /><br />One.<br /><br />The Jeep speeds up as it drives erratically, then crashes into a mound of rocks.<br /><br />***<br /><br />Ash woke up... another nightmare, if you can call a memory a nightmare.<br /><br />He rubbed his eyes, and glanced to his wrist.<br /><br />Red numbers flooded his vision, 3:00 am.<br /><br />He rolled over and went back to sleep.<br /><br /><br /><br />Day Dreams<br /><br /><br />Brightness greeted Eta when she aroused from a drug induced stupor. At least, that's what she assumed as she was coming to, based on the physical effects she was feeling... she must have relapsed again, blacked out...<br /><br />"I wonder what bedroom I've wondered into this time..." she thought to herself.<br /><br />Eta's muscles were sore and she felt the hard surface underneath, it felt cool on her back. It must have been a kitchen floor, maybe tile, or hardwood?<br /><br />As she squinted, she saw simply bright white... "It must have been one of 'those' partiesâ€¦", she mumbled to herself as she grimaced past sore muscles to get to her feet.<br /><br />As the fog cleared she found the resolve to sit up, find her bearings, look around.<br /><br />Something didn't set right.<br /><br />Her mind rebooted; Eta became keenly aware that nothing was right. This was no frat house, and she wasn't in college anymore. That girl has been dead for many decades, and forgotten. At least she tried to forget.<br /><br />Drawing her groggy attention back to the situation at hand her training started to kick in and she began to assess.<br /><br />There were a large series of L.E.D. lights above, too bright to look directly at. The ceiling was high, too high to reach. The room wasn't very big, square, maybe twenty feet. She realized that her clothes had been changed and she was wearing something akin to a white leotard or bicycle suit, with black trim.<br /><br />She kind of hoped to keep it when she was out of here. It was a nice contrast to her bright red hair and permanently tanned skin; one of the benefits of having such a mixed family heritage.<br /><br />Eta scanned the room, no doors, windows, no escape. Only flat walls, she now realized were metal, as far as she could tell. In the center of the room was a white table and one box.<br /><br />She approached carefully and opened the box, assuming this must be the next step in whatever weird situation she'd found herself.<br /><br />"They said training would involve untried methods. Maybe this is a training center and my next assignment. 'Escape the box with no escape "<br /><br />Wellâ€¦ maybe she wasn't as good at catchy mission names as she was with two Glocks at her side.<br /><br />When Eta opened the box all she found was a plastic bag, with an iPhone. She turned it on.<br /><br />It booted silently...<br /><br />***<br /><br />She woke suddenly, but silently. As though she'd been awake for hours.<br /><br />Dawn crept through the blinds and brushed the edges of the wall in hues of pink and orange.<br /><br />Laying there, she picked a spot on the ceiling and let her mind defog.<br /><br />The only thing weirder than waking from a weird dream, is waking inside of a weird dream, only to then wake from that dreamâ€¦ But that was who Eta was after allâ€¦ weird was her middle name. <br /><br />Well, OK, maybe not. But it sounded cooler than "Technical Sergeant Nadine Etaine Romanakov", or "Lieutenant" as she was now known by the Colorado Police Department.<br /><br />" 'Eta'... It's pronounced "Eh'---tuh", she'd had to be deliberate in her pronunciation with a probie the other day. He was probably just trying to get to her because of her odd accent or because she was a chick. Either way it worked, but he probably wished it hadn't when she was done with him. You don't get to be one of the first females in the USAF to enter combat, or afterward onto the SWAT team, as a female, without being a bit feisty. She didnâ€™t mean to make him cower, but she just enjoyed a good argument so much.<br /><br />Eta glanced at the bare walls of her studio apartment. Eight years out of the service. Except for an inspirational quote by the front door, she still hadn't bought a single painting, or decoration for the room. This was not home, there is no home. This was a safe houseâ€¦ an eight year safe house. <br /><br />â€œFind your happy place girlâ€¦â€ She closed her eyes to meditate.<br /><br />A butterfly lands on the window sill. A feeling of warmth flooded over her for just a moment.<br /><br />Her attention was broken by three short buzzes.<br /><br />The phone. "coffee?"<br /><br />Janice was the one human odd enough to actually want Eta's company outside of work. She went to the obligatory bar nights with the crew, and a few parties now and then, but generally she stayed to herself. She wanted the interaction, but didnâ€™t trust it. So few would push back at her intellectually. They were usually so taken by either her figure (if male) or her brawn (if female) that few would engage her mind. She kept up the persona at work, was the life of the party on many weekends, but never really let anyone in.<br /><br />Janice asked for three years before she finally said yes, but nowâ€¦ it was a nice distraction. Janice was an engaged mind, and was always willing to call Eta out on a factual error. It was like hanging out with a walking encyclopedia. Maybe, she'd be willing to call Janice a friend?<br /><br />She typed back, "Sure, be there in 10." She strapped on her dual Glocks, rib holstered, and a jacket to cover.<br /><br />You never can be too careful.<br /><br />***<br /><br />Out the door into the wild white winter madness that is Colorado Springs.<br /><br />Over at the cafe, Janice is still chattering away about the latest episode of Blacklist, which Eta is normally very into. Today she just feels less normal.<br /><br />That dream, it had gone away since she'd been doing her herbal teas, but now it was back. Was this a relapse?<br /><br />Suddenly, Eta's world disappeared.<br /><br />In her mind's eye, she saw a waiter walk by and trip on her purse, dropping the bottle of Perrier he was carrying, shattering all over the floor, contents pouring over her shoes and spraying up onto Janiceâ€™s lap.<br /><br />She turned to Janice who had stopped talking, and was now staring at Eta. â€œYou feeling' OK Hun?â€<br /><br />"I'm fine Janice, sorryâ€¦ these darn headaches, what were you saying?â€ Eta denied.<br /><br />"You need something? I always carry the standards, generic of courseâ€¦ same thing, less moneyâ€¦"<br /><br />"Janice, I'm fine, really." Just then a waiter walked by with a bottle of Perrier. He tripped on Janiceâ€™s purse, and Eta reached out and caught the bottle before it hit the ground.<br /><br />"Whoa! Did you see what you just did?â€ Janice pushed up her glasses. â€œYou have amazing reflexes." She was always expressing how impressed she was with Eta. She worked in Dispatch, but told Eta that she always dreamed of being on SWAT like her.<br /><br />&nbsp;"Thank you." the waiter said, and he moved on looking embarrassed.<br /><br />"Wow girl." Janice said. "You really are good.â€ Janice went back to recanting the television show.<br /><br />Eta tried her best to appear interested, she actually really enjoyed the show. But these day dreams, or whatever they are, were getting out of control. It wasn't so bad at first. A few harmless day dreams, followed by coincidences. But after eight years, they were getting hard to manage.<br /><br />Janice glanced over at the TV, a commercial about laundry soap. At the end, a butterfly floats out of the towel and across the room, landing on the window sill.<br /><br />"Hmm..." Eta said under her breath.<br /><br />Then, Janice disappeared again.<br /><br />***<br /><br />Eta was somewhere else, far away. City street. Cars driving by. People walking along, minding their own business, most looking down at screens as if they were walking by GPS.<br /><br />Now there's people running, the streets roped off, and she can see people in jogging suits, numbers on their back.<br /><br />Then her attention is drawn to a young man placing a backpack in the trash can.<br /><br />She knows this place, but she's never been here.<br /><br />A blinding flash of light. Glass splattering all over her.<br /><br />The percussion hits her chest andâ€¦<br /><br />***<br /><br />It's over... This one is not new. It's the same day dream she's been having for weeks now. Two in a matter of minutes? Itâ€™s getting worse.<br /><br />Janice didnâ€™t seem to notice this time. She was going on about Lizâ€™s baby being raised without her.<br /><br />"Oh my!" she hears from just behind her, she turns to see a couple staring at the bar. Several others are slowly turning to see the television as well. <br /><br />"An explosion went off at the Boston Marathon just moments agoâ€¦" the reporter covered the information they had so far.<br /><br />"â€¦wait, did they say Boston?" A sick feeling comes over her... is that even possible?<br /><br />Glancing at the TV, she sees the young man with the back pack, from her day dream, running away from the scene in the news with all the other people.<br /><br />Eta runs her hands through her hair, and stares at the T.V.<br /><br />"What is happening?" Eta whispers. "Excuse me, Janice, I have to make a call. I have friends in that area. I'll see you later OK?"<br /><br />Without waiting for an answer, she hurries out the door, then walks to a nearby pay-phone, which are getting harder to come by these days, keeping her face from a clear line of sight to any cameras she sees.<br /><br />She calls the F.B.I. hotline with an anonymous tip regarding the bomber, and the news channel that had the photo.<br /><br />Then she heads homeâ€¦ to process.<br /><br /><br />White Noise<br /><br /><br />Bright blinding white permeated the room. A white cardboard box sat on a thin snow white metallic table in a small space covered in white painted titanium walls.<br /><br />"How did I get here?â€ Ash said.<br /><br />No reply.<br /><br />Looking down he noticed the white form-fitting uniform he was wearing. His pale southern sunburn looked worse in this light. Despite years in hot arid climates, he could never hold a tan.<br /><br />A soft hiss was coming from the ceiling, but he couldnâ€™t look up because the ceiling was lit from end to end with bright LEDâ€™s, air conditioner maybe. He could barely see around him without squinting. Disorientation started to settle down after the first few moments awake, and he found his bearings.<br /><br />Thoughts move to business, take stock. Name/Rank: "Captain Therun Ashland, USAFâ€, he said out loud. No response. Taking inventory of his body, every part was accounted for, legs, arms, toes, fingers, the scar on his neck under his right ear. No pains, other than the usual. <span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span><br /><br />Ash tried to recall his last memory before finding himself in this room void of any obvious doors, windows, or openings of any kind, not even a seem was apparent as he circled the room with his eyes and fingers. The thoughts came like rapid fire. He didnâ€™t bother with escape plans, not yet anyway, obviously he'd been handled before arriving here and must be under surveillance this very moment. No way to defend against attack without knowing where the openings are, but there would be no use because if he were going to be killed he would be in some dank prison cell not this white washed room in some overly tailored biking suit. Still, better to be defensive and keep himself against a wall ready to react.<br /><br />â€œBring it in Ash, bring yourself togetherâ€¦ What is the last thing you recall? I was on X-Island doing survival training in the South Pacific. I had just dropped in from 15,000 feet. Working through the forest to the hacienda I had retaken the residence and recovered the â€œstolenâ€ technology, sent it back up on a UAV. Routine training mission. I swam off island to a nearby island and was waiting for my evac. There was a flash of light..."<br /><br />â€¦thatâ€™s it, there was no other memory. He woke up here.<br /><br />The box on the table was the only thing in the room with him, besides the table itself, which seems to be built into the floor. He approached the table cautiously. Eyes had adjusted some to the light, but there was still not much to see. The legs and table appeared to be built of high strength metal, not breakable by hand. The package seemed like any other white shipping box, sealed in white duct tape end to end.<br /><br />The table was cool to the touch, not cold, but cool, like everything else in this room, floor, walls, etc. Ash pealed at the tape slowly, ready to react to its contents. This box was obviously here for him, it was the only thing in the room, and no-one had reacted to his being awake. Laying the tape aside he opened the lid and found an iPhone, neatly wrapped in plastic. He opened the bag and turned on the phone.<br /><br />It booted silentlyâ€¦<br /><br />***<br /><br />Ash woke up in a sweatâ€¦ it was that stupid dream again! The same dreamâ€¦ for 8 years. PTSD came in all shapes and sizesâ€¦ for Theron Ashland it came in the form of recurring dreams, nightmares really, and hallucinations of cloud beings on certain people. That was an odd symptom too.<br /><br />Some doctors told him it was suppressed memory, and the visions due to &nbsp;neurological damage.<br /><br />Others told him that it was his mind trying to make sense of the missing time, and adjusting to life in the "normal world" where people aren't trying to kill you. Many vets are paranoid of attacks at any moment.<br /><br />He wasn't sure himself.<br /><br />Regaining sleep was unlikely. The clock read 4:47 am. Ash had a few hours before he had to head to the school to start his day full of studentsâ€™ excuses for misbehaving, and professorsâ€™ excuses for their students test results. Things were so much simpler when you could just shoot your enemy. How he ever agreed to accept a position involving people, he'd never understand.<br /><br />Still, a steady paycheck in post military retirement was more than many Vet's hoped for. Maybe some extra calisthenics would help erase the bizarre and bring him back to realityâ€¦ again.<br /><br />***<br /><br />By 6:00 am, Ash was heading to the school for another day, and he stopped off at the Grounded Cafe for a do-over from last night.<br /><br />As he walked in, Mike, the usual morning Barista nodded "I'll have it right up for you Ash. It's on the house today, thanks for keeping us safe last night man. I heard all about itâ€¦"<br /><br />Ash nodded back, "Thanks Mike! I'm a little out of practice thoughâ€¦ that little old man is stronger than he looks." They both laughed.<br /><br />Ash took a seat in his favorite leather chair, in his corner. He was skimming through the local paper, when he noticed another man come and sit in the chair beside him. He tried to ignore him, but he could see from his peripheral that the man was just staring straight at him. He couldn't focus to read a single headline, and so he put the paper down on his lap and smiled at the man.<br /><br />Small stature, balding in the front, large square glasses. "How can I help you misterâ€¦?"<br /><br />"Briar. Dr. Briar. I was most impressed with your show of heroism last night Airman. I apologize for calling you Marine, I know how you boys like to keep yourselves distinguished from each other. The thing is, I've never seen a member of the Air Force fight like that. Pardon me for saying, but I've always thought of you Airman as more brains than brawn, if you know what I mean." Dr. Briar smiled, warmly.<br /><br />"No offense taken, I've just had more than my fair share of run-ins, and I've picked a few fights with those Marines too."<br /><br />"And what might your name be?"<br /><br />"Ash, just call me Ash."<br /><br />"Thank you. Tell me Ash, I couldn't help but notice that you were looking at the man rather odd, just before he jumped on you. It was not the face of strange curiosity that most of the bystanders had. There was somethingâ€¦ unique... about the way you looked at him? What was that about?"<br /><br />Images of the dark cloud, the figure, the small frantic creature danced in his head for a moment. Ash passed it off, "Dr. Briarâ€¦ is it? I suppose I was just gearing up for the fight. Those of us with training tend to view conflict a bit different from other folks." <br /><br />"Ash!" Mike the Barista was calling.<br /><br />Turning to Dr. Briar, "Coffee's readyâ€¦ I have to go to work now."<br /><br />"I seeâ€¦ well, thank you for satisfying my curiosity." Dr. Briar stood, and put out his hand. Ash stood, and took it with a firm shake.<br /><br />"So long Dr. Briar, nice to meet you." Ash grabbed his coffee and headed out the door.<br /><br /><br />Eventful Day<br /><br /><br />Eta woke up at 9:09 am that morning, as usual. Her day started out normal enough. Then during a raid on a local drug den, she was almost knocked over by some punk kid who couldn't have been more than 22 years old.<br /><br />He got away!<br /><br />How could she have been so careless? A vision of the kid passing her, just moments before he passed her, had her so distracted that she let him slip right by.<br /><br />As the inconvenient day dream predicted.<br /><br />It was as though she couldnâ€™t move. Like she was frozen.<br /><br />She felt so stupid.<br /><br />Boss Man said that it could happen to anyone, but she knew she wasn't OK. Something felt very wrong about it. She let her team down, and a suspect got away. Of course, she didn't tell him about the day dream or temporary paralysis, so for all he knew she just missed him. She needed to take time to clear her head, before someone got hurt.<br /><br />They'd been begging her to use her time off anyway, she'd accrued the stuff for two years straight without using any. So she took a paid leave for six weeks.<br /><br />That ought to be enough time for this to pass over...<br /><br />Or drive her nuts.<br /><br />One way or the other, this will be settled.<br /><br />Eta placed her keys on one of the hooks by the door, and her purse on the other. If you can call a Kevlar fanny pack a purse.<br /><br />She locked the dead bolt, three times. Set the alarm code, 3. 3. 3.<br /><br />Eta glanced to the inspirational photo of a waterfall, framed on the wall, a constant reminder,<br /><br />She read the inscription many times, "Nine represents achievement and completion, when all has been put right, and in order. Order is control. Control is power. Power is defense. Master Xua Chi".<br /><br />Order is Control is Power is Defense.<br /><br />Defense against the world, people, but mostly, herself. Self cannot be trusted. Above all else, self must be controlled.<br /><br />Eta glanced the place over, everything is where she left it. Windows and doors secure. Sounds correct. Smells check.<br /><br />She let out a sigh, shoulders relaxed.<br /><br />Glocks were placed on the bed, under the pillow, still in their holsters. Eta walked to the kitchen, poured hot water into a teacup, then parked herself at the desk by the window.<br /><br />The desk was close enough for light and fresh air, but just out of the line of sight from other tall buildings and potential sniper nests.<br /><br />She rotated the blinds, so that no one could see in.<br /><br />The screen came to life, the cursor blinking in the long rectangular box.<br /><br />Eta tried searching for anything to make sense of her situation. Besides, research and facts always calmed her nerves.<br /><br />"day dreams"<br /><br />"visions"<br /><br />"psychic"<br /><br />"psychotic breakdown"<br /><br />Every search brought with it a rabbit trail of charlatan websites looking to make money off of the unsuspecting fools who dared to believe their trickery.<br /><br />No real science.<br /><br />No exploration.<br /><br />Nothing to explain her situation.<br /><br />"What is happening to me?" Eta sighed. The sights and sounds of the bombing were fresh in her mind. The news channels didn't do it justice.<br /><br />She'd been there... somehow. She could smell the explosive materials, she could see the bombers face when he put the backpack in the trash can. He was frightened, not bold. He looked at the people around him so sadly. Then he walked away, head down, crying.<br /><br />They caught him this morning, thanks in part to her tip off, she hoped. He looked surprised on the T.V. Like he didn't know how he'd been caught.<br /><br />This does not bode well for the order she'd crafted for her life. There had to be logical rational answers for this phenomena. Or was this just the past catching up with her. Something finally snapped inside, and she'd gone nuts after all?<br /><br />What if I'm in a padded cell right now, and my dreams are attempts to wake me up to that reality?<br /><br />â€œHa!" She forced a laugh.<br /><br />After hours of nothing concrete coming up in the searches, Eta closed the laptop lid, and sat back in the chair. She rubbed her neck, and eyes.<br /><br />They burned, she must have forgotten to blink again. Eta laid down on the bed, and stared at the ceiling until sleep overtook her.<br /><br />***<br /><br />She's late for an appointment. What appointment? She doesn't know. She's just sitting here in the food court, with her laptop, waiting for... someone?<br /><br />The sign on the wall says Sky Mall.<br /><br />She's been here a while, she's not sure how long. Time ticks on, clicking away on the keyboard, search after search brings no relief.<br /><br />She looks down and there are lockers. A man walks up, black jeans, black jacket with a Carhart logo, gray beany. He opens the locker, and pulls some things out.<br /><br />Just as he is about to turn around, there is a flash of bright light, and he is gone. No bomb this time, he's just gone.<br /><br />She needs to meet him.<br /><br />But why?<br /><br />***<br /><br />Eta sat up. Had she been asleep?<br /><br />Not sure.<br /><br />Another dream to become real in the future?<br /><br />Maybe?<br /><br />Yes. Definitely Yes.<br /><br />Probably yes?<br /><br />â€œI'm so tired..." She mumbled as she worked her way to her feet.<br /><br />She'd pulled $3,000.00 from savings, on the way home last night. She'd planned to drop by the airport and take the first one-way flight to anywhere but here.<br /><br />"Hey Cortana", she said. "Show me Sky Mall"<br /><br />The laptop buzzed to life. A computer voice spoke back to her; "Here is a list of relevant searches for Sky Mall. The most relevant is Sky Mall in Los Angeles, CA."<br /><br />A few taps on the keys revealed pictures of the mall on Bing Images, which showed lockers that matched the ones from her dream, and a cafeteria which also bore an eerie resemblance to her dream.<br /><br />Ten minutes from Union Station, and L.A.X.<br /><br />Perfect, she could walk from the airport.<br /><br />"I guess L.A. counts as 'anywhere but here'..." Eta said.<br /><br />After a quick phone call, Janice was more than willing to drop her off at the airport on one condition.<br /><br />"Take pictures, and bring me back an amazing story of your adventures! And if it's boring... Just make something up for me!"<br /><br />She looked out the plane window, it wasn't as impressive without the thrill of a parachute strapped to your back.<br /><br />Eta closed her eyes, and sleep came, eventually.<br /><br />Terminate<br /><br />Dr. Briar watched Ash leave, as he sat back down to his tablet at the table in the back of the Cafe. He stared down at his screen, which showed vital signs playing alongside a recording of the event as it had unfolded the night before. Dr. Briar pulled up the private Instant Message, and began typing.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Status Report:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject A reacted to the white noise, even 10 years after the end of his experiment. Elevated vital signs, and increased erratic behavior were nearly instant.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B showed signs of elevated vitals, but he was attacked, which could be the reason. Subjectively, I feel he saw something; however, even after my interview with him this morning, the data was inconclusive.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Status Recommendation:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject A: Terminate.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B: Observe.<br /><br />Dr. Briar pressed send, and took a sip of his Carmel Macchiato.<br /><br />For a few minutes the cursor remained empty, blinking on the screen as he watched the sea of humanity. If they only knew. Is this what it was like for the dinosaurs? Just roaming about their territory, eating and pursuing each other until one day an explosion changed everything?<br /><br />One of his experiments, the Problem Child, had managed to elude them, despite their precautions. They thought they knew who he was. Until they realized that the person they thought they had, didn't exist. By then it was too late.<br /><br />He'd shown great promise during the experiments. His brain scans were clean, and showed remarkable openness to the experiment. Text book case study, all they'd hoped.<br /><br />Maybe that was why they didn't look deeper, spend more time digging into the layers of his background. Not that it was entirely their fault. The boy had laid down an iron clad identity, bearing scrutiny at multiple levels. He probably could have passed most background checks with it.<br /><br />The only problem was that he didn't exist. When they went to find him for a check-in, he was missing, and as they began to dig deeper into his past, the alias turned out to be empty.<br /><br />Ryan Wrecking never existed.<br /><br />They couldn't find him, but they could put a stop to the madness before it got any further out of hand. Already they'd eliminated 25 of the most extreme cases, the ones gone wrong, the liabilities. They must put a lid on this mess before there was any more exposure to the program.<br /><br />A message popped up.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Assign:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject A: Terminate.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B: Terminate.<br /><br />Dr. Briar may have been many things, but he was assuming that taking the life of a man who was not showing signs of being a danger to himself or others was just beyond his moral line. He responded.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Response:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B: Terminate Not Needed.<br /><br />The reply came immediately.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Assign:<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span><br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B: Terminate!<br /><br />He didn't need to be reminded, again, who was really in control. With sadness, he resigned himself to the inevitable.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Response:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Understood.<br /><br />He sent a text to the associates he had brought to town.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Assign:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject A - Terminate.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Follow Assign:<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B - Terminate.<br /><br />A few moments later came a reply.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Understood.<br /><br />Dr. Briar suddenly felt ill.<br /><br />It's hard to imagine that just a few short years ago, he'd been a respected scientist. Sure, he worked on the fringe of science. But how had he come to this? Reduced to putting down his own test subjects?<br /><br />This was the price he'd paid to touch the edges of the known universe and rip them open. This was the price he'd paid to have his funding needs met by an "interested party". &nbsp;Only too late did he realize, too interested.<br /><br />Dr. Briar put his tablet and paperwork away, and stood up to leave. He put the cell phone in a metallic lined bag, separate from the battery. He tossed them in separate trash cans a block down the road. He dug his heel into the SIM card.<br /><br />He took out another phone from his pocket, turned it on. He scrolled through the list of contacts. He sent one final text.<br /><br />There.<br /><br />Maybe one good deed could start to make up for the horrible actions heâ€™d taken thus far.<br /><br />He destroyed that phone another block down the road, then headed up to his hotel for a well earned nap.<br /><br /><br />Negative Noise<br /><br /><br />The iPhone had one short recording.<br /><br />"You are in no danger. We are conducting an experiment in sound waves and sound deprivation. After a cleansing period to be determined, you will be ushered into another room. You are to sit in the chair for another period of time, also to be determined. That is all. Within a few weeks, you will leave no worse for the ware, unless this works. In which case, you will be better for the ware." <span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span><br /><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span><br />That was all it said. The one and only track, ended.<br /><br />It was always bright. The lights never turned off.<br /><br />It may have been 10-15 days? He was trying to keep marking the days on the white box, but he could not tell when one day started, and another ended. With no natural light as reference, his circadian rhythms were off.<br /><br />Ash wakes to the sound of his food being delivered for breakfast. Food, his only measuring stick. He eats in silence. He doesn't even hear the static from above anymore. When he is done he places his tray on the shelf and it disappears into the wall again.<br /><br />*Click* The hissing in the overhead turns off.<br /><br />"Wellâ€¦ that's new." Ash said the first words he'd spoken out loud in days.<br /><br />The air pressure changes.<br /><br />&nbsp;A slight crack in the wall snaps open.<br /><br />The crack opens wider as the panel disappears into the wall.<br /><br />*Whoosh*, the crack becomes a doorway, which opens into a long hall.<br /><br />Since this is the first change he's had since he woke up in the loony bin, he decided it was better to investigate the new, than stay in the old. Ash makes his way down the long hall, lit with blue LED's this time.They are flashing forward, like a runway.<br /><br />He makes his way into a room, approximately 15 by 15.<br /><br />The door shuts behind him.<br /><br />The floor is built from a hatch work of solid wire. Very sturdy; however, unnerving as you can see several feet beneath the floor which is covered in deep triangular foam, of various sizes and depths. The walls, ceiling, all made of the same substance. One chair, bolted to a frame on the floor. It looks comfortable enough. Like a dentist chair.<br /><br />Seeing no other option, he takes a seat.<br /><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span><br />The lights turn off.<br /><br />The only thing Ash could see was the flashes of white dots dancing around his eyes from having been in the light moments earlier.<br /><br />The silence is deafening.<br /><br />Ash clapped his hand once, and noticed that the clap was hollow, as though the walls took the sound and sucked it out away from him, then wouldn't give it back. If it were possible for sound to be less than nothing, this was it. All he could hear was his own breathing.<br /><br />After about ten minutes, his ears began ringing. It started subtle, but grew worse.<br /><br />When the ringing finally subsided, he could hear the beating of his own heart, and the sloshing of bodily fluids throughout his own body.<br /><br />Alone with his thoughts. He could almost hear them.That same familiar voice thatâ€™s always there but barely noticed.<br /><br />'watch out for that...'<br /><br />'don't say that...'<br /><br />'oh man, what did you do that for?'<br /><br />It was as though the inner voice became almost audible. As though he could hear himself think. Or rather as though his thoughts could speak to him audibly. It was an odd sensation, but occupying his mind was the only thing he could do. He'd never admit it to a captor, but solitary confinement had been favorite part of being a prisoner of war. He was only caught once, and only for a few months. But in a way he enjoyed the self-time, the silence.<br /><br />Now though, alone in the dark, with no external sound of any kind. He sits, eyes closed, listening to nothing. Literally the closest to nothing anyone's ever listened to. Even alone back on the island, you actually heard... Something. Birds, wind, waves. He was now listening to NOTHING.<br /><br />He did not know how long he'd been asleep, or if he'd been asleep, but his concentration suddenly snapped into focus with the feeling that he was not alone in this pitch black room.<br /><br />He'd known before he entered the room that cameras were on him, and he was being observed... but that wasn't it.<br /><br />He felt the air in the room change, as though someone had entered the room with him.<br /><br />He could hear breathing in the room.<br /><br />He wasn't tied to the chair, he could get up, but where to? He couldn't see, he'd memorized the layout though. He knew the entrance he came from, and he didn't see any other known entry ways before the door shut out the light.<br /><br />Ash slowed his breathing, and observed with his four remaining senses for any clue as to the whereabouts, and intention, of the new participant.<br /><br />"Ash..." a voice whispered from all around him.<br /><br />"Ash... " He sat up straighter.<br /><br />"Theron... I know about you... I know who you are... Fraud! Hero? Youâ€™re no hero! Killer!" The voice was filled with pain, and anger.<br /><br />Ash shakes his head... the voice vanishes.<br /><br />For the first time since entering this crazy place, Ash felt the cold ice of fear creep into his spine.<br /><br />The voiceâ€¦ almost sounded likeâ€¦ her?<br /><br />Images flashed through his mind of the wars he'd fought, the people he'd killed for God and country.<br /><br />Was it for God? Who's god? The god of good oil prices? What country?<br /><br />The one that left him to rot in an Iranian prison, because we could not admit we had been there to begin with? Disavowed they called it. Escape required cruel and unusual punishment of his captors, even for him.<br /><br />Her.<br /><br />Images of his sister floated through his mind. He went into the military because of her. She died so young. He'd protect and serve others, because he couldn't defend her. What would she think of him now?<br /><br />Killer or Protector?<br /><br />Proud, Disappointed, Horrified?<br /><br />"Ash...."<br /><br />A single tear crawled out of his eye, rolling down his cheek.<br /><br />Through the silence a buzzing grew steadily... startling him<br /><br />Bzz.<br /><br />Bzz.<br /><br />***<br /><br />Ash woke up. Still in his hammock. He pulled his phone from the pocket of his jacket next to him. 3:17 am, 1 new message from an unknown number.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>"ATTACK IS IMMINENT!!!!â€<br /><br />Ash felt his senses come alive, 25 years of drills and real life base attacks had drilled that message into his DNA. His focus came alive, and his hearing and sight became acute. Ash steadied his breath. He didn't move, not yet, he wanted to know what direction?<br /><br />What attack? In Oregon? Really? He started to assume that this was a prank being pulled by one of the new cadets. He got out of the hammock and walked to the kitchen to get some water. He still didn't feel right though. Something was off. He placed the glass on the counter andâ€¦<br /><br />Ash heard the front door open, and then close softly. It could be a prank, and if so, he didn't want to accidentally shoot one of his students. At the same time, that is not a message to be sent lightly.<br /><br />He quietly pulled a side arm from the drawer, but holstered it in the back of his sweat pants.<br /><br />He also grabbed his favorite Shun Chef Knife from the butcher block on the counter.<br /><br />Ducking down behind the island, he used the glass reflection to watch the hallway. Two men entered the living room, geared up in all black, using professional hand signals. They moved silently, and they were built for battle. These were no students. Ash watched as they made their way to the porch, where his hammock still looked like it was being slept in. Intended to fool students pulling a prank, but just as handy when being attacked for real.<br /><br />He started to rise, so that he could work his way to a better defensive position, then a bright light made him pull back. He couldn't see anything for a few moments. When the light cleared, he could see the men on the porch as they approached the hammock, and then opened fire on it.<br /><br />Ash pulled his side arm out of his waist band. He took several shallow breaths, followed by one deep breath, steadied his focus.<br /><br />He could see the hammock, and its contents falling apart. He stood, worked quickly to get behind the couch, which he had special made for just such an occasion. Steel and Kevlar make a great barrier to hide behind.<br /><br />You never can be too paranoid... or is that prepared? He could never tell the difference.<br /><br />He crouched behind, on one knee, and set the knife on the floor. With both hands to steady his aim, he fired several shots into the nearest target. The assailant flailed forward into his counterpart and then went over the balcony.<br /><br />Stunned by the commotion, the other remaining member turned to see where the shots came from, too late.<br /><br />Ash emptied the remaining rounds and number two fell back, half over the railing himself. The weapon fell to the floor.<br /><br />Ash re-loaded a second clip, and approached with caution. He kicked the assailants weapon aside, and checked for life. DOA. A glance over the edge revealed no change of survival there either.<br /><br />Ash grabbed the man's rifle, slung it over his shoulder, and swept the house for more.<br /><br />Clear.<br /><br />This was an AN-94, advanced Russian assault rifle, way too expensive for general issue to the Russian military or police.<br /><br />This was a special breed that had the brass to enter the house of Theron Ashland.<br /><br />Who sent them? What in the world did they want with a professor? Surely it can't be a grudge from his special forces days? That was over eight years ago, then again, people like Ash never really retire. They just go dormant, to be pulled back in a moment's notice.<br /><br />Which is why he still had a Commanding Office to report to quarterly.<br /><br />When he knew the coast was clear, he called his C.O. to report the incident. No reason for the civilians of this sleepy town to know that there had been a Russian attack on US Soil, they'll send a sanitation team to make it look like it never happened.<br /><br />"General Akbarâ€¦ Sir this can't be an isolated incident. I need to go dark and find out what's going on."<br /><br />"Understood. Check back in two weeks, and we'll see what we can find on our end. We'll let the school know we called you into active duty on short term notice. They won't ask any questions. And Ashâ€¦ be careful. I donâ€™t like the smell of this."<br /><br />"Iâ€™m with you on that. I'll check back in two weeks."<br /><br />Ash threw together his hiking pack, and necessities. He pulled his cash from the stow-away hidden in the back of the couch. He took one last glance at his cabin, now a little less beautiful than beforeâ€¦ sighed, and walked out the door. The sanitation team would be here before light. And he needed to be gone by then too. Ash pulled the bike out from the garage.<br /><br />He didn't ride the Vulcan often, but he really enjoyed it.<br /><br />"I really do need to get out more, even if it takes people looking to assassinate me to get me out of town." A small bird flew away.<br /><br />He glanced both directions. There were redundancies in Washington and California, that only he knew about. He didnâ€™t even report these to his C.O.<br /><br />"Heck, let's make like the birds and head south for the winter".<br /><br />Ash pointed the nose down the road, and took off. Making sure to keep to back roads, but only those with multiple exit paths and trails he could take the bike on if needed.<br /><br />"Russiansâ€¦" he pondered, "what do the Russians want?"<br /><br /><br />Failed<br /><br /><br />Morning came too early.<br /><br />Dr. Briar checked for messages from his team. Then he realized the mistake heâ€™d made by sending that final text.<br /><br />He was too late to do anything about his people. The military authorities were already covering Ash's house. They weren't really his people anyway, they were the Benefactor's. They were not loyal to him. They were growing too comfortable.<br /><br />But what would the Benefactor say?<br /><br />It was always assumed that something could go wrong during a termination; therefore, he had always taken every precaution. Including paying his assistants in cash only, no paycheck to tie them back to him.Texting apps with self-deleting time tables of 15 minutes.<br /><br />When he sent the text, he thought Ash would see them coming and escape, not stay and take them out. But it's fair game, and these two were not nice men. It might be nice to start over.<br /><br />Dr. Briar pulled up the private Instant Message, and began typing.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject A: Terminated<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Subject B: Termination Failed, Escaped, On the run. Cannot find him. Lost two assistants.<br /><br />There was a long silence. Butterfly's flew around in his belly, and his hands grew cold. The Benefactor, as he was called, did not take kindly to failure; however, he was also a cold calculator. He was hard, but not rash. As long as Dr. Briar was still valuable he would not receive his direct wrath.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Return, regroup.<br /><br />A wave of relief washed over him. He was still valuable, for now.<br /><br />â—<span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;"> </span>Heard<br /><br />He picked up his normal phone, and pressed speed dial.<br /><br />"Welcome to Esion, this is Clara speaking, how may I help you?"<br /><br />"Clara, this is Dr. Briar. Get my plane ready, I'm leaving in one hour... Oh, and hire two more assistants, I seem to have lost mine."<br /><br />"Yes sir Dr. Briar, right away." &nbsp;He pressed the big red button, and re-holstered the phone. Clara, even she was brought in by the Benefactor. Dr. Briar was not the owner of his company anymore, just a cog in its wheel. She would process his termination with the same pleasant disposition she used to order his coffee.<br /><br />He wondered if she would be out here herself, if Dr. Briar's unique medical expertise were no longer deemed necessary.<br /><br />He sat in the cafe for a few moments, pondering his next moves. He would not be allowed to fail again.<br /><br /><br /><br />Mystery Man<br /><br /><br /><br />The Los Angeles Sky Mall was bustling with humanity, roaming about like so many ants building a colony without knowing why.<br /><br />Eta sat in the food court just outside the Chick-fil-A, overlooking the railing down to the first floor, in perfect view of the lockers. She couldn't get the latest vision out of her head. After that incident last week with the Boston Marathon, she was officially freaking out.<br /><br />Then this new vision started up.<br /><br />She's sitting in this exact spot, and a some guy in black jeans, a black jacket with a Carhart logo, and a gray beany steps up to a locker, he turns around ... Vision Over.<br /><br />"I'm not sure who this guy is" she said out loud "... all I know is, if he has any clues, about why I'm seeing stuff before it happens, it's worth a shot to come find out."<br /><br />Eta shook her head, "You're talking to yourself again girl, get a gripâ€¦"<br /><br />So she waitsâ€¦ and waits. She's been here all day, every day, with her laptop surfing the Wi-Fi... waiting. She's had only this vision, every night, for a week since the Boston Bombing. So here she sits, waiting.<br /><br />Day 8â€¦ hotels and fast food.<br /><br />Bouncing from site to site, Google providing no solid answers, she's attempting to use the other search engines, hoping for different results. She's hit up Bing, Yahoo, DuckDuckGo... she's moved on to other searches. Checked out the Library of Congress, National Security Archive, Virtual Library, Infomine... grasping for answers.<br /><br />There were a few articles about paranormal studies and research.<br /><br />Did she hear voices?<br /><br />No.<br /><br />See things she can't explain?<br /><br />Yes.<br /><br />Did she have the feeling she was being watched by a departed loved one?<br /><br />No.<br /><br />She laughed so abruptly a few people turned around to look at her for a moment.<br /><br />There were articles about people coming back from war with strange mental reactions, and being diagnosed with PTSD.<br /><br />Paranormal research was hard to wade through, for every non-biased scientific evaluation, she found 1,000's of kooks and quacks that ranged from television psychics to religious evangelists. All of them wanting to provide you with answers, for the low cost of... or a love gift of...<br /><br />Eta was on her fourth cup of Venti Soy Chai Tea Latte for the day when she saw him. Out of the corner of her eye, there was a man with dark blue jeans, nearly black. A black jacket with a Carhart label on the shoulder, and a gray beany... he was headed in the direction of the lockers.<br /><br />She took a deep breath and held it. Pulse quickened. Muscles tensed.<br /><br />Eta tried to look casual, in case he turned her direction.<br /><br />If Special Forces training had taught her anything it was how to observe without being seen. He was headed straight for the locker... and then passed it, and headed out of sight down the halls to the bathrooms. She wouldn't have the time to get down there and follow, and the vision was about this locker, so she decided against her inner desire to pursue and kept up with her searches. If that was the guy, he'd be back. If it wasn't, she'd miss the real person she was here to see.<br /><br />More searches turning interesting tidbits of useless data. It's a decent distraction, but she's no closer to the answer. Eta stares into the crowds below as they meander through the mall. Out of the corner of her eye she sees the man again, walking away from the lockers. He disappears underneath her, toward the exit. She debated, "Should I go follow, or stay and wait?" She decides that the vision has her in this seat, and him at that locker.<br /><br />"Hello Ms.?"<br /><br />Eta is startled by a young man holding a heart shaped box. "Yes?"<br /><br />"This is for you, have a great day", the young man starts to take off.<br /><br />"Wait, sirâ€¦ this can't be for me." He turns back to face her. "I don't know anyone here. You got the wrong person."<br /><br />"No Ma'am. You are the one. He pointed to you, black leather jacket, laptop, purple hair. He said to give this to you." he turned and walked away.<br /><br />Eta looked around, she felt the icy tinge run through her spine. She could feel her breathing quicken, and her skin tighten. Glancing through the crowd, she could not locate any immediate danger. Eta put her ear to the box to listen for mechanical devices. It was light, too light to be a bomb.<br /><br />She opened it, and there was a note inside.<br /><br />"Why did you open this box? Haven't I taught you anything? Gotcha! Over Please." She recognized the hand writing instantly. "Hey there slugger! Almost didn't notice you there. What are you doing here?" <br /><br />It may have been eight years, but they were partners for longer than that. She stood up, glancing around. She didn't see him. She looked toward the locker, there he was. The man in the black jacket pulled a backpack from the locker, and turned around.<br /><br />"Of all the crazyâ€¦ ", she said. Her muscles relaxed. Eta folded her arms, feigning annoyance, and nodded her head calling him over.<br /><br />The she sat down. She took a deep breath, and sighed.<br /><br />She watched him ride the escalator.<br /><br />He's getting older, more gray on the sides.<br /><br />Therun â€œAshâ€ Ashland walked up to her, smiling. He'd aged well. "Time has been hard on you, huh tough guy?"<br /><br />"It's done wonders for you slugger. Look at you. Purple huh?"<br /><br />â€œWellâ€¦ I was going for blue, but since my hair is bright red, it came out more purple.â€<br /><br />â€œIt looks good on you.â€ Ash sat down, and recanted his ordeal in Oregon, and his trip down here to pull his backup safety stash of cash (can't be hacked or tracked), weapons, and passports. The part about odd sightings was unusual, but made her feel better about her condition.<br /><br />She recanted her precognitions (she learned a new word while she was waiting) and experiences in Colorado.<br /><br />When they had both finished explaining their individual reason for being there, she sat back and blew a strand of purple from her face.<br /><br />"Psychic precognitionâ€¦ huh? Weird."<br /><br />"I'd have taken your Russians and over my complete psychotic breakdown the other day, more predictable. The cloud pets don't sound sound so annoying."<br /><br />"I guess, if you say so.â€<br /><br />â€œIf are having issues tooâ€¦ could this be related toâ€¦â€<br /><br />â€œMaybe? Letâ€™s not jump to any conclusions yet. What do you do next?" Ash sat back in his chair.<br /><br />"I don't know. I've been searching for answers, Google has been most helpful. Did you know I could have latent Psychic Tendencies?" She smiled.<br /><br />"Really?" Ash put on his best serious face. "Wellâ€¦ let's get you your own program!"<br /><br />They both chuckled.<br /><br />"So Chief, what's the plan now for you?"<br /><br />"I don't know, but I do know I'll need someplace to crash tonight. Then I get a game plan together."<br /><br />"You mean we, right?"<br /><br />"I don't want to drag you into my mess, I've got people after me."<br /><br />"My precognition let me straight to you. You'll need a partner watching your back. Looks like you're stuck with me, old timer."<br /><br />"You have any visions of a 5 star hotel in our future? We can't use credit cards, got to stay off the grid."<br /><br />"No. The last vision was of you standing there at that locker, of course I didnâ€™t know it was you at the time. Fresh out of visions for the momentâ€¦. Waitâ€¦ it's coming in nowâ€¦"<br /><br />Ash sat up a little straighter, after his fight with the homeless man he was open to strange possibilities.<br /><br />"I see you, and meâ€¦ sleeping in a cardboard box!" She laughed.<br /><br />Ash slouched a little, and shook his head. "Well, we can't stay here. But I think I can afford someplace better than a cardboard box."<br /><br /><br />Dissonance<br /><br /><br /><br />They went to Denny's for a bite to eat. You can always count on Denny's when you are operating on a budget. And when you don't know how long you'll need to stay off the grid, any amount of cash needed to be budgeted.<br /><br />Before they could make it into the front door, Ash heard something, and motioned for Eta to follow, making their way to the alley. As they rounded the corner they heard voices, and they didn't sound pleasant.<br /><br />Curiosity got the better of Ash who peaked into the alley to assess the situation.<br /><br />Eta made a grumpy face, but followed. "Ash" she whispered, "we're trying to stay off the grid. Let's not get in the middle of something we'll need to explain to police."<br /><br />He motioned to keep her voice down, and glanced around the corner. Threeâ€¦ no four, Latino men, armed with hand guns. All dressed in sagging jean shorts, white ribbed under shirts, and button down plaid, with only the top button together. Blue bandannas wrapped around their arms.<br /><br />"Chester, Chesterâ€¦ it's been a long time since we've seen you around. You ain't buying no more, or what?" The smallest guy was doing the talking. Shaved head, snake tattoo on his neck running up and over his skull. "You still owe us that program you promised boy. The one that would get us into people's accounts. Where'd you disappear to essay?!"<br /><br />Turning to the chunky guy on his left, "Gordo, what's it been, twoâ€¦ three years since we've seen this punk? Since he promised us a program in exchange for our merchandise? I think it's time to pay up Sancha!"<br /><br />Ash could see the shadowy figures rising up and around the four guys. Never quite solid, but so often present.<br /><br />Then a light caught his eye. He noticed the gleam first, and then the whole scene exploded into bright light.<br /><br />Ash backed away, temporarily blinded.<br /><br />After a few blinks, he could see a very large man, brown hair, dressed in all white, with a gold belt and long sword. He was standing behind the victim, but looking straight at Ash.<br /><br />Another large man, similar to the first, stood beside him. He smiled, motioned to the four assailants, nodded approval, thenâ€¦ both large men disappeared.<br /><br />Ash motioned to Eta. Four assailants. Armed. One victim. Cover me, from over there. I'm going in.<br /><br />Eta pulled her side arm, and sighed. "Really?" she whispered, "I took vacation time to come to LA and shoot gangsters?" <br /><br />Eta silently made her way to just behind the parked car. She stayed in the shadows between a parked car and the wall, but kept her side arm pointed at the group.<br /><br />Ash adjusted his chest holster within easy reach of his slightly open jacket, and walked down the alley.<br /><br />He walked right up next to the largest of the bunch and stood there for a while. They were so busy being idiots they didn't even notice him.<br /><br />Amateurs.<br /><br />Tired of being ignored; "Fellas, what's with the party!?" he screamed out with his best impression of a loud drunk.<br /><br />Snake head whipped around, followed by everyone else. Tiny looked the most surprised, considering Ash was standing right next to him and he didn't notice. Snake head put on his best bravado stance.<br /><br />"You lost boracho? I think you need to move alongâ€¦ "<br /><br />"Why? I was just leaving the bar and my friend disappeared. I was looking all over for you buddy, where did you go?" Motioning at the victim.<br /><br />Snake head didn't give him time to respond. "You have 60 seconds to leave, before you become a part of this party amigoâ€¦ and trust me, you don't want to be a part of this party."<br /><br />â€œParty!â€Ash yelled. â€œI like parties.â€ Ash straightened up a little, still putting on a drunk show. "I'll tell you what amigoâ€¦." He swallowed, dramatically for show. "I'll give you and your friends 10 seconds to leave this alley before I ruin your party and take my friend with me."<br /><br />Snake head had enough; "Tinyâ€¦ get rid of this guy, huh?" Tiny pointed his gun at Ash's headâ€¦ and the world slowed down.<br /><br />Ash moved in to Tiny's space, pushing the gun away from this body, stepped on his toes, pulled his knee into the opposite inner thigh, simultaneously taking his gun, and hitting him in the nose with the butt of the gun.<br /><br />With one big shove he knocked Tiny into the guy behind him and they both went down.<br /><br />Ash whipped around, in two quick strides and hit the third guy in the throat so hard that he dropped his gun and went down to the ground with both hands on his neck trying to breathe.<br /><br />Finally, he side stepped to Snake Head, and with one hand took Snake Headâ€™s gun hand and held it pointed to the ground, and with the other hand holding the gun he'd just taken from tiny, pointed it right between his eyes.<br /><br />By this point two of the assailants were still nursing wounds while the third had recovered and was starting to point his weapon at Ash.<br /><br />Just then the gun flew out of the third guys hand, as a faint swoosh could be heard from down the alley. He grabbed his hand in immense pain as it was now bleeding profusely.<br /><br />Ash, no longer pretending to be a drunk, looked Snake Head directly in the eye.<br /><br />"So here's how this goes. A) you and your friends leave your weapons in the alley and take your friend to the hospital to have his hand looked at. B) you all leave here in body bags as my friend back there picks you off like ducks at a carnival shooting gallery."<br />Snake Head hesitated for a few moments, then dropped his gun, as did the others. The four of them ran out of the alley, right past Eta who stayed hidden so they wouldn't see where she'd fired from.<br /><br />Ash picked up the weapons and put on the safeties before loading two into his belt. He threw the others to Eta who had come down the alley by now, sure that the four had left for good.<br /><br />"You're getting rusty old man." &nbsp;Eta half smiled.<br /><br />â€œAnd youâ€™re still trigger happy. That guy wasnâ€™t going to shoot me when Iâ€™ve got a gun to his leaders temple.â€<br /><br />â€œYou canâ€™t know that. Street gangs are not known for their quick wit and logic. Not in any country.â€<br /><br />â€œGood point.â€<br /><br />â€œBesidesâ€¦ I didnâ€™t come out here to lose you, I still need answersâ€¦â€<br /><br />Ash returned a smirk and then turned to their new friend. "You have a name kid?"<br /><br />"Chesterâ€¦" he managed, a bit shaken from the encounter. "Kid. That's funny." Smiling, but still looking a bit dazed.<br /><br />"Well Chester, we'll need to get moving before they gather the courage to return with the rest of their gang, or police come after someone reports the commotion. Anyplace you know that we can go and be safe for a bit?" Ash said.<br /><br />"And maybe some food?" Eta shrugged, "This kind of interrupted our dinner plans."<br /><br />"Yeahâ€¦ sureâ€¦ this wayâ€¦" Chester motioned to the street and the three of them left the alley.<br /><br />***<br /><br />They followed Chester on the bike, and made their way to the Union Gospel Mission.<br /><br />Chester waived to the night guard, "Albert. These two are with me."<br /><br />"OK, sounds good. Just sign them in Mr. Chester." The guard was older, he looked like he could hold his own, but he wasn't going on any marathons any time soon either.<br /><br />"Who were those guys?" Eta asked as they ate a free meal.<br /><br />"It's a long story. Suffice it to say that they were part of a life I no longer live. By the way, not that I'm not gratefulâ€¦ but where did you two learn to do that CIA, Kung Fu, Ninja stuff back there?"<br /><br />Eta and Ash glanced at each other and smiled.<br /><br />"It's a long storyâ€¦" Ash stated.<br /><br />Chester smiled. "I see what you did there."<br /><br />Eta kept her eyes on the doors and windows.<br /><br />Chester followed her glance, "Well hey, seriously, if you want to avoid prying eyes, the mission might not be the best place for you two to stay tonight. Most of the residents are long term bums, and are still discussing the conspiracy behind the Kennedy assassination, or Roswell. But we do get a few Savvy's in now and then, and if your faces are on the news or anything, they might know. They'd take the opportunity for a tip line reward."<br /><br />Chester glanced at the wall for a moment, and turned back to them. "Why don't you stay at my place? It's big enough."<br /><br />"Are you sure it's a good idea to invite total strangers to your house Chester?" Ash put up his hands, "Not that I mind."<br /><br />"Listen. Strange you are. Weirdâ€¦. Maybe even a little scary. But two people who would walk into an alley to save a guy they don't know with super cool Ninja moves, and then let the crooks live? If you meant me harm, we wouldn't be here right now. Besidesâ€¦ there's something about youâ€¦ I can't put my finger on it, but I like you. I always trust my gut. Itâ€™s always done me right. Besides it's the least I can do after you risked your lives to save me. Win-Win in my book."<br /><br />"Good enough for me. It's a viable option. What do you say Eta?"<br /><br />"Less cameras to avoid at his place than the hotels" she shrugged,â€ Better plan than any".<br /><br />They wrapped up their dinner, Chester waived to Albert on the way out, and the three headed to his place.<br /><br /><br /><br /><br />New Precognition<br /><br /><br /><br />Chester is giving them the grand tour. â€œKitchen is to the left. There is a Kurieg there, if you drink coffee. There's also a latte wand if you are the frills type.â€<br /><br />Ash is following just behind her, she can feel his warmth after coming in from the cold.<br /><br />â€œNope, not going there. Get a grip girl.â€ She said under her breath.<br /><br />Chester pointed to a big room just off the landing, â€œThe movie theater is set up with a touch screen display menu, just scroll through the movie you want to see.â€<br /><br />â€œWaitâ€¦â€ Eta said, â€œDid you say movie theater?â€<br /><br />â€œYes I did.â€ Chester shrugged. A grin barely escaped the corner of his mouth, â€œIf you close the doors you can turn the volume up as loud as you want it. It's totally sound proof from the rest of the house." He smiled big about that one.<br /><br />â€œSome house you got here Chester.â€ Ash threw a glance at her, eyebrows raised.<br /><br />â€œThanks, follow me.â€<br /><br />Heading up the stairs, then down the hall to the right, they passed several doors. Pointing to Ash, "You can take the first door, and Eta, you can take the next one. Just so you know, my working hours are just starting, so I'll most likely be asleep when you two wake up.<br /><br />"Mi casa es Su' casa. Iâ€™m usually up about mid-morning to noon. If you need a computer there is a guest computer in the living room. I'll let you get settled, I'll be in my office on the third floor if anyone needs me."<br /><br />Chester nodded, and headed up the second flight of stairs.<br /><br />"Who is this guy?" Eta said.<br /><br />"I don't knowâ€¦ but I think we signed up for the wrong career." Ash said. "I'll see you in the morning".<br /><br />Eta watched him head into his room, and then opened the door to her own.<br /><br />Room? A suite was more like it.<br /><br />It had its own bathtub with jets, and empty walk-in closet. She put her bag on the chaise, then laid across the bed. "Wow, this is a really nice mattress too."<br /><br />***<br /><br />As she laid on the bed, she had the sensation of floating... Like she was on a raft in the middle of the sea.<br /><br />Suddenly, she was not in the room anymore. She was floating above the house. The wind was whipping through her.<br /><br />She wanted to see closer to the ground, and found herself floating to wherever she willed.<br /><br />At ground level, the light was still on in the house. Chester was working on something.<br /><br />She thought of Ash, and found herself floating through the windows, doors, walls, and into his room.<br /><br />Ash was sitting on a chair, by the window. His hand under his chin, as he stared outside. He seemed to be lost in thought.<br /><br />An image filled her mind.<br /><br />A tower.<br /><br />Then she was whisked away from the house, and went flying through the air. She was passing over houses at sonic speeds. She was tempted to put her arms out like superwoman, but she was just along for the ride.<br /><br />As her flight slowed, she came upon a gated compound. There was a manicured lawn at the entrance, and private security.<br /><br />Multiple buildings on the property, but the most interesting was a large tower, 13 stories tall.<br /><br />At the top of the tower, a light was on through a corner window.<br /><br />Eta floated to the top of the building, and saw a man with white hair, writing something in a notebook on his desk. He turned and looked in her direction, but he didn't see her. He just stared outside while he spoke into his Bluetooth headset.<br /><br />Floating down, and then backward, she was hurled through the night, back into her room.<br /><br /><br />***<br /><br />Finally, she was laying in her bed again, staring at the ceiling.<br /><br />She sucked in a deep breath, and let it out slowly.<br /><br />It's getting worse... these pre-whatever's... she needs answers.<br /><br />Glancing at the clock, it's 11:02 am. She over slept.<br /><br />Overslept is an overstatement when you have no plans, but she felt like it was over sleeping.<br /><br />She dressed in a hurry and worked her way down the hall. The smell of food carried her down the stairs and around the corner. Ash was flipping pancakes and stirring sausage.<br /><br />Chester was sitting at the counter talkingâ€¦ then he turned around to see Eta walk in.<br /><br />"Hey. I'm not normally awake quite this early, but Ash discovered cookware, and I followed the scent down stairs. I didn't even know that stuff was in there!"<br /><br />"I'm glad he found it, I'm starving." Eta gave a nod to Chester, and sat down at one of the bar stools.<br /><br />Ash turned to her, "You OK Slugger? I heard you tossing and turning a bit last night."<br /><br />"Yeah, I'm alright. &nbsp;Butâ€¦"<br /><br />Ash turned back to her. "What is it?"<br /><br />"I had another one."<br /><br />"Really? What about this time?"<br /><br />Eta glanced at Chester and hesitated.<br /><br />"It's OKâ€¦I trust him. I had a buddy run a background on our friend here. No offense Chester." Chester smiled and waived it off, mouth full of coffee, "And it turns out that Chester's security clearance is almost as high as ours"<br /><br />Eta gave Chester a glance. "OK, what do you do? Exactly, I mean?"<br /><br />"A little of this, a little of that." Chester laughed. "Here's the thing." Chester pulls out his business card and hands it to Eta. "Internet Data and Program Security Analyst - Chester Jandihar IDAPSA".<br /><br />"Wow", she said, rolling the title in her mouth silently.<br /><br />"Yeah, it's a mouthful. But CEO's get all freaked out when you call yourself a Hacker, so the business title makes them feel better. Basically, I design programs of various kinds for businesses, non-profits, even the government. I also test systems for vulnerabilities and report my findings. Then in my free time I just build random stuff, programs mostly." Chester shrugged, "So as I said, a little of this, and little of that."<br /><br />Ash smiled, "He's a regular consultant for the N.S.A."<br /><br />Eta raised an eyebrow in disbelief.
